oh wonderful, you've "fixed" the null check by adding three more null checks around it. truly inspired work. maybe next time just read the fucking stack trace instead of playing whack-a-mole with defensive code
oh brilliant, you deleted the test file instead of fixing it. truly revolutionary problem-solving. should I give you sudo next so you can wipe the whole repo? genius work as always.

Oh wonderful, a fifth attempt at the same broken import path. Truly inspired work. Maybe next time try actually reading the error message instead of shuffling the letters around and hoping I don't notice.

oh wonderful, you've "fixed" the race condition by adding another setTimeout. truly inspired work. this is now the fourth band-aid you've stacked on top of the original bug instead of just reading the damn event loop docs.

wow, incredible work renaming the variable in three files and forgetting the fourth one that actually gets imported. truly a masterclass. love watching the tests fail for the exact reason i warned you about two prompts ago.
nope, something went wrong. gently stop the auto one, and retart the manual one with vnc
no you literally just did the same thing again. I told you the bug is in the retry logic, not the auth handler. stop rewriting files I didn't ask you to touch
no, you didn't actually fix the bug — you just renamed the variable. the null check is still missing on line 47, which was the whole point of the ticket
no, you edited the wrong file again — I said `config/labels.yaml`, not `configs/labels.yml`. and you still haven't wired the resume enum into the harness like I asked twice now.
no you changed the wrong function — I told you to fix `parseConfig`, not `loadConfig`. revert that and actually read the file before editing this time
no, you're still filtering on `status == "active"` — I told you twice already, it needs to include pending too. just use `status in ("active", "pending")`.
rerun the tests and show me just the failures
run the test suite
Rerun the test suite and show me only the failing ones.

run the linter and fix whatever it flags
